summary: Keep the runtime version declaration in root.go (var version = "2026.6.2", matching the published entry and library convention) instead of the 4.27.0 generator's new version.go; drop the standalone declaration from version.go and inline the MCP server version literal.
reason: The library's verify-release-ledger guard flags any added/removed 'var version =' line for an existing CLI (post-merge automation owns version stamping). The 4.27.0 generator moved the version var from root.go to a new version.go and added one to cmd/<cli>-pp-mcp/main.go; on a reprint of a CLI last printed pre-4.27, that shows as moved/added version lines and fails the guard. Aligning to the established library layout (version in root.go, MCP literal) preserves the stamped value and passes the guard.
validated_outcome: go build ./... passes; 'version' prints 2026.6.2; git diff vs origin/main shows no added/removed 'var version =' line in any runtime-version-candidate file.
upstream_issue: generator 4.27.0 version.go placement breaks the library release-ledger guard for cross-version reprints
